preset_description: Dark = identity signal. Visual language expresses who the user is. Validator gets authority and weight (heavy dark). Mover gets minimal light with one dark CTA punch. Explorer gets all-neutral — open, spacious, no urgency.

brief.audience: SaaS trial Users for an Intent-Based, AI-Ready Design System

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: Global wayfinding with a minimal variant — strip nav links and keep a single persistent CTA (e.g., 'Start Trial'). At the decision stage, a Mover needs zero friction and one clear escape hatch into conversion, not exploratory links.

sections.name: Hero — Single Action Directive
sections.rationale: HeroPrimary in Mover variant opens the page with one headline and one CTA — no subhead, no secondary action. At the decision stage the visitor already understands the product; the job is to compress intent into immediate action. The with-social-proof sub-variant can place a LogoStrip directly beneath the CTA to add silent credibility without slowing the Mover down.

sections.name: Silent Credibility Bar
sections.rationale: SocialProofBar in logos-only Mover variant sits immediately below the hero. No headline, no quotes — just recognizable brand marks. For a decision-stage Mover, this delivers trust in under a second without demanding attention or reading time.

sections.name: Outcome Metrics
sections.rationale: StatBlock in Mover variant (3-stat, no sources, no headline) delivers pure metric impact — e.g., '47% faster design handoff,' '3× component reuse.' At the decision stage, Movers convert on proof of momentum and scale, not detailed methodology. Numbers do the persuading.

sections.name: Pricing — Clear Plan Selection
sections.rationale: PricingCard is the core decision-stage conversion mechanism. Mover variant strips descriptions down to price and CTA only, with one highlighted recommended plan. The Mover doesn't compare features line-by-line; they need to see the number, confirm it's reasonable, and click.

sections.name: Mid-Page Conversion Prompt
sections.rationale: CTABanner in Mover variant (strong headline, single CTA, no subhead) inserted after pricing to capture users who've made their decision but didn't click a PricingCard CTA. At the decision stage this acts as a safety net — a second, unmissable conversion surface for the action-oriented Mover.

sections.name: Footer with Recovery CTA
sections.rationale: FooterMinimal in with-cta variant recaptures departing Movers who scrolled past all prior conversion points. On a decision-stage page, every exit is a lost trial signup; the recovery CTA is the last-chance mechanism tuned for this archetype's bias toward quick action.

page.reasoning: minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only. Decision-stage Mover users need frictionless wayfinding toward trial conversion.

Mover variant suppresses subhead and cta_secondary — one message, one action. with-social-proof variant places LogoStrip immediately below CTACluster, establishing credibility for trial decision without cognitive load.

slots.headline: Ship faster with an AI-ready design system.

page.reasoning: Mover variant is logos-only, no headline — silent credibility. logos-only is the highest-frequency placement; drop directly below HeroPrimary on awareness pages.
Mover variant suppresses sources and headline; pure metric impact. 3-stat is the preferred default for decision-stage users who need outcome validation without friction.

stats.label: Average time to first working screen

page.reasoning: Mover variant strips description; price and CTA only. Trial conversion requires immediate clarity on plan cost and action, minimizing decision friction.
Mover variant: strong headline, single CTA, no subhead. minimal for high-frequency use — above footer, between sections. Decision-stage Movers respond to direct, uncluttered conversion prompts.

slots.headline: Your trial ends soon. Lock in Pro today.

page.reasoning: with-cta variant recaptures departing users on key conversion pages — use on pricing and signup flows. Critical recovery mechanism for trial conversion at funnel bottom.
